title: Sequential ChIP-Seq
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"h05d5","text":"ChIP-Seq has been used extensively to profile genome-wide transcription factor binding and post-translational histone modifications. A sequential ChIP assay determines the in vivo co-localization of two proteins to the same genomic locus. In this chapter, we combine the two protocols in Sequential ChIP-Seq, a method for identifying genome-wide sites of in vivo protein co-occupancy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dcq6b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
